solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int numMusicPlaylists(int n, int goal, int k) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[][] dp = new int[n + 1][goal + 1];

        dp[0][0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            for (int j = 1; j <= goal; j++) {
                dp[i][j] = dp[i - 1][j - 1] % MOD; // Add a new song
                if (i > k) {
                    dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j] + (long) dp[i][j - 1] * (i - k)) % MOD); // Repeat a song
                }
            }
        }
        return dp[n][goal];
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * goal)
space complexity: O(n * goal)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to count the number of valid playlists. The key idea is to define `dp[i][j]` as the number of playlists of length `j` using exactly `i` unique songs. We build the solution by considering two cases: adding a new song or repeating a song (only if `k` other songs have been played). The recurrence relation ensures all constraints are met efficiently.
